title: Ensure that the admin.conf file permissions are set to 600

input_row.title: Ensure that the admin.conf file permissions are set to 600
input_row.description: Ensure that the admin.conf file has permissions of 600 .
input_row.rationale: The admin.conf is the administrator kubeconfig file defining various settings for the administration of the cluster. This file contains private key and respective certificate allowed to fully manage the cluster. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity and confidentiality of the file. The file should be readable and writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: Run the following command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf Verify that the permissions are 600 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod 600 /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf
input_row.default_value: By default, admin.conf has permissions of 600 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubeconfig_admin_conf_permissions

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl exec -n kube-system <control-plane-pod-name> -- stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf` to check the permissions of the `admin.conf` file on the control plane node.
- The expected output should be `600` or more restrictive (e.g., `400`, `500`).
- If the output is `600` or more restrictive, the check passes; otherwise, it fails.
